source_files: Fremantle 2025 Segmentation AI-Ready Format 2026_05 V2.xlsx (sheets: Persona Info, Urban Hipster)

profile.Profile: "Urban Hipster" fans are socially active, trend-conscious individuals who enjoy exploring new experiences and maintaining a stylish, modern lifestyle. They are career-focused and value independence, often spending on dining out, fitness, travel, and technology that enhances their image and convenience. With a strong preference for authenticity, they favour brands that align with their values of sustainability and creativity.
profile.Consumer Attributes:
"Urban Hipster" fans:
Trend-Conscious: Keeps up with the latest fashion, technology, and cultural trends, often setting themselves apart with a distinctive style.
Experience-Seeking: Prefers spending on experiences like dining out, live music, and travel rather than material possessions.
Socially Active: Highly engaged in social activities, both in-person and online, valuing connections and building their personal brand.
Career-Driven: Focused on professional growth and financial independence, often investing in further education or career-enhancing tools.
Sustainability-Oriented: Favours brands and products that are environmentally friendly, ethically produced, and socially conscious.
Tech-Savvy: Quick to adopt new technology, especially if it enhances their lifestyle or aligns with their interests in creativity and convenience.
Urban Lifestyle: Enjoys the perks of inner-city living, such as proximity to cafes, cultural events, and boutique shopping.
Authenticity-Focused: Values originality and uniqueness in brands, favouring local, artisanal, or niche offerings over mainstream options.
profile.Bio: Rebecca was introduced to the Dockers by her dad Steve when she was 5 years old, and it quickly became part her DNA. She loves the club and loves the social side of being part of the Purple army and attending most games with her best mates. But sometimes work and life can get so busy that there just isn't enough time to go to the footy. Rebecca worked in Melbourne for a few years after graduating, before heading back to her beloved Fremantle, away from Melbourne winters and back with family and friends. Working as a People & Culture manager in an ad agency keeps Rebecca very busy, but not so busy that she doesn't have time for fun with friends and exploring new life experiences. Rebecca is a career woman who enjoys being financially independent and in control over her future. Rebecca rents an apartment in Northbridge close to work, her gym, pubs, bars, restaurants, and most importantly the beach. Life is good and the only thing that could make it better would be a Freo premiership!
profile.About Me:
I love being part of the Purple Army
I enjoy being the centre of attention 
I'm always looking for new things to experience
I set ambitious personal goals
Being able to enjoy the finer things in life is important to me.
profile.Location: Inner urban reasonably affluent area living in a house
profile.Lifestyle: Exercises at gym regularly; Uses ride share / taxis; Invests in further education; Holiday Traveler; Career oriented
profile.High Spend Categories: Body Corp / Rent Fees; Tertiary Education Fees; Sports participation; Public Transport / Taxis; Gym Health Club Fees; Overseas / Local holidays

profile.Personal Attitudes: I want the feeling of power that having money can bring; I like to impress others by displaying signs of affluence; I pursue status and power; I am still trying to work out what I want from life; I want to be admired for my achievements; I can be a bit unreliable at times; I adopt the latest technologies and use them easily; I want my life to be full of excitement; I maintain a high level of physical fitness; I enjoy doing things that are risky or extreme; I try to get as much as I can while putting in the least possible effort; I go out of my way to look stylish and attractive

profile.Buying Behaviour: Best quality, regardless of price; Like to see others with new technology before buying; Most Technically advanced; Online reviews as a guide; Uses BNPL
profile.Cultural Diversity: Moderate to High: Urban Hipsters members have a moderate to high level of cultural diversity with good representation of people born outside Australia, second generation Australians, and / or have worked overseas. Urban Hipsters have a higher percentage of males to the Australian population and slightly more than the Fremantle member base. Religious affiliations are also diverse which is consistent with the cultural background of Urban Hipsters members.
profile.Sustainability: High: Urban Hipsters members have a high level of awareness and interest in Sustainability and invest time to ensure they are well informed. Socially aware and environmentally conscious, they are most likely to volunteer, join environmental movements, and back politicians on their green credentials - extending their values into ethical investing, structured fitness, and mindful practices like yoga and meditation.
profile.Political Values: Moderate to Progressive: Urban Hipsters members take a genuine but measured interest in politics - progressive in outlook and drawn to parties with strong environmental and social credentials, without being vocal or dogmatic about their views. When it comes to casting a vote, backing politicians who champion green policy and deliver meaningful social good is what matters most to this segment.
profile.Outlook on Life: Positive: Urban Hipsters members have a pretty positive Outlook on Life and see themselves as generally pretty lucky to enjoy the life they lead, which they think is better off than others. They are generally very good in health and lead an active and healthy lifestyle. Urban Hipsters members are optimistic about the future both for themselves and the world and look forward to what's ahead.

knowledge.title: Identity & demographics
knowledge.text:
Persona: Urban Hipster (representative member: Rebecca)
Profile: "Urban Hipster" fans are socially active, trend-conscious individuals who enjoy exploring new experiences and maintaining a stylish, modern lifestyle. They are career-focused and value independence, often spending on dining out, fitness, travel, and technology that enhances their image and convenience. With a strong preference for authenticity, they favour brands that align with their values of sustainability and creativity.
Consumer attributes: "Urban Hipster" fans:
Trend-Conscious: Keeps up with the latest fashion, technology, and cultural trends, often setting themselves apart with a distinctive style.
Experience-Seeking: Prefers spending on experiences like dining out, live music, and travel rather than material possessions.
Socially Active: Highly engaged in social activities, both in-person and online, valuing connections and building their personal brand.
Career-Driven: Focused on professional growth and financial independence, often investing in further education or career-enhancing tools.
Sustainability-Oriented: Favours brands and products that are environmentally friendly, ethically produced, and socially conscious.
Tech-Savvy: Quick to adopt new technology, especially if it enhances their lifestyle or aligns with their interests in creativity and convenience.
Urban Lifestyle: Enjoys the perks of inner-city living, such as proximity to cafes, cultural events, and boutique shopping.
Authenticity-Focused: Values originality and uniqueness in brands, favouring local, artisanal, or niche offerings over mainstream options.
Average age: 31 (segment skews 25-34)
Relationship: Single or Couple without kids
Average SES: 66 / 100
Occupation: Business professional, manager student
Location: Inner urban reasonably affluent area living in a house
Lifestyle: Exercises at gym regularly; Uses ride share / taxis; Invests in further education; Holiday Traveler; Career oriented
Share of Fremantle members: 13%
Gender skew: 40% female

knowledge.title: Bio - Rebecca
knowledge.text: Rebecca was introduced to the Dockers by her dad Steve when she was 5 years old, and it quickly became part her DNA. She loves the club and loves the social side of being part of the Purple army and attending most games with her best mates. But sometimes work and life can get so busy that there just isn't enough time to go to the footy. Rebecca worked in Melbourne for a few years after graduating, before heading back to her beloved Fremantle, away from Melbourne winters and back with family and friends. Working as a People & Culture manager in an ad agency keeps Rebecca very busy, but not so busy that she doesn't have time for fun with friends and exploring new life experiences. Rebecca is a career woman who enjoys being financially independent and in control over her future. Rebecca rents an apartment in Northbridge close to work, her gym, pubs, bars, restaurants, and most importantly the beach. Life is good and the only thing that could make it better would be a Freo premiership!

knowledge.title: Cultural, sustainability & outlook
knowledge.text:
Cultural diversity: Moderate to High: Urban Hipsters members have a moderate to high level of cultural diversity with good representation of people born outside Australia, second generation Australians, and / or have worked overseas. Urban Hipsters have a higher percentage of males to the Australian population and slightly more than the Fremantle member base. Religious affiliations are also diverse which is consistent with the cultural background of Urban Hipsters members.
Sustainability: High: Urban Hipsters members have a high level of awareness and interest in Sustainability and invest time to ensure they are well informed. Socially aware and environmentally conscious, they are most likely to volunteer, join environmental movements, and back politicians on their green credentials - extending their values into ethical investing, structured fitness, and mindful practices like yoga and meditation.
Political values: Moderate to Progressive: Urban Hipsters members take a genuine but measured interest in politics - progressive in outlook and drawn to parties with strong environmental and social credentials, without being vocal or dogmatic about their views. When it comes to casting a vote, backing politicians who champion green policy and deliver meaningful social good is what matters most to this segment.
Outlook on life: Positive: Urban Hipsters members have a pretty positive Outlook on Life and see themselves as generally pretty lucky to enjoy the life they lead, which they think is better off than others. They are generally very good in health and lead an active and healthy lifestyle. Urban Hipsters members are optimistic about the future both for themselves and the world and look forward to what's ahead.

knowledge.title: Membership movement 2024-2025
knowledge.text:
Urban Hipsters
Commercial In Confidence
Urban Hipsters demonstrated the most amount of movement with the highest percentage of non-renewing members overall recording a net decline of 2%.
Growth in new and/or returning members was comparable with the volume of members who churned. 
Urban Hipsters recorded the highest churn of all segments at 47%, more than double the club baseline (22%).
This segment has shown YOY decline as a % of the base, suggesting deeper analysis of the causal factors driving churn is required.
I'm Rebecca...
Membership Movements from 2024 - 2025

raw_slides.text:
executive summary
The Fremantle Dockers member base is large, loyal at its core, and growing - but unevenly. Seven distinct segments reveal meaningful differences in tenure, attendance, churn risk and commercial value. Overall membership grew strongly in 2025, yet the growth masks a critical divide: three segments are delivering stability and value while two high-volume younger segments are leaking members at rates that significantly exceed the club baseline. Retention is the defining challenge and clearest commercial opportunity.
Segmentation is a growth and risk-management tool designed to enable smarter acquisition, stronger retention and develop more valuable partnerships.
What we found
What this means
Strategic Priorities
Member value, loyalty and engagement are not evenly distributed across the base
Overall membership grew, but Urban Hipster declined in share as churn outpaced acquisition
A quarter of members with game access attended zero games presenting a potential retention risk
Fremantle members over-index on EV, financial services, travel, and premium lifestyle spend - high-value partner territory
A single retention strategy is insufficient. Each member segment requires different interventions at different lifecycle stages
Urban Hipsters and Gen Fun members cannot be treated as a single acquisition pipeline - they need early, personalised engagement before they lapse
Non-attending members require a non-match value proposition
The commercial audience is valuable and highly targetable
Protect the base: Implement early-tenure intervention programs targeting high churn segments
Engage non-attenders: Build a demonstrable non-match value offer
Grow the engine: Prioritise retention in core segments - Modern Families and Happy Suburbanites 
Sell smarter: Use segment-level spend propensity and lifestyle alignment to build evidence-based partner propositions

Urban Hipsters
Commercial In Confidence
"Urban Hipster" fans are socially active, trend-conscious individuals who enjoy exploring new experiences and maintaining a stylish, modern lifestyle. They are career-focused and value independence, often spending on dining out, fitness, travel, and technology that enhances their image and convenience. With a strong preference for authenticity, they favour brands that align with their values of sustainability and creativity.
I'm Rebecca...
"Urban Hipster" fans:
Trend-Conscious: Keeps up with the latest fashion, technology, and cultural trends, often setting themselves apart with a distinctive style.
Experience-Seeking: Prefers spending on experiences like dining out, live music, and travel rather than material possessions.
Socially Active: Highly engaged in social activities, both in-person and online, valuing connections and building their personal brand.
Career-Driven: Focused on professional growth and financial independence, often investing in further education or career-enhancing tools.
Sustainability-Oriented: Favours brands and products that are environmentally friendly, ethically produced, and socially conscious.
Tech-Savvy: Quick to adopt new technology, especially if it enhances their lifestyle or aligns with their interests in creativity and convenience.
Urban Lifestyle: Enjoys the perks of inner-city living, such as proximity to cafes, cultural events, and boutique shopping.
Authenticity-Focused: Values originality and uniqueness in brands, favouring local, artisanal, or niche offerings over mainstream options.

Urban Hipsters
Commercial In Confidence
Urban Hipsters demonstrated the most amount of movement with the highest percentage of non-renewing members overall recording a net decline of 2%.
Growth in new and/or returning members was comparable with the volume of members who churned. 
Urban Hipsters recorded the highest churn of all segments at 47%, more than double the club baseline (22%).
This segment has shown YOY decline as a % of the base, suggesting deeper analysis of the causal factors driving churn is required.
I'm Rebecca...

Further analysis of the changes in the rban Hipsters segment identified a small shift in composition of members by age, with many more members now having an age.
The table to the right highlights the change in age composition within this segment showing a slight influx of older members.
This segment is still heavily centred on 25 to 34-year-olds.

< 25 | +3.6% | The increase in younger segments is consistent with the theory that most new member sign-ups are a younger profile.
We saw some migrations from the Urban Hipster segment with additional information including age being appended during the year.
This explains why there were some migrations from the Urban Hipsters segment to the Gen Fun segment.

Urban Hipsters
Commercial In Confidence
I'm Rebecca...

I'm Rebecca...
BIO
Rebecca was introduced to the Dockers by her dad Steve when she was 5 years old, and it quickly became part her DNA. She loves the club and loves the social side of being part of the Purple army and attending most games with her best mates. But sometimes work and life can get so busy that there just isn't enough time to go to the footy. Rebecca worked in Melbourne for a few years after graduating, before heading back to her beloved Fremantle, away from Melbourne winters and back with family and friends. Working as a People & Culture manager in an ad agency keeps Rebecca very busy, but not so busy that she doesn't have time for fun with friends and exploring new life experiences. Rebecca is a career woman who enjoys being financially independent and in control over her future. Rebecca rents an apartment in Northbridge close to work, her gym, pubs, bars, restaurants, and most importantly the beach. Life is good and the only thing that could make it better would be a Freo premiership!

Values and Attitudes Dashboard
Cultural Diversity
Sustainability
Political Values
Outlook on Life
Urban Hipsters members have a moderate to high level of cultural diversity with good representation of people born outside Australia, second generation Australians, and / or have worked overseas. Urban Hipsters have a higher percentage of males to the Australian population and slightly more than the Fremantle member base. Religious affiliations are also diverse which is consistent with the cultural background of Urban Hipsters members.
Moderate to High
High
Moderate to Progressive
Urban Hipsters members have a high level of awareness and interest in Sustainability and invest time to ensure they are well informed. Socially aware and environmentally conscious, they are most likely to volunteer, join environmental movements, and back politicians on their green credentials - extending their values into ethical investing, structured fitness, and mindful practices like yoga and meditation.
Urban Hipsters members take a genuine but measured interest in politics - progressive in outlook and drawn to parties with strong environmental and social credentials, without being vocal or dogmatic about their views. When it comes to casting a vote, backing politicians who champion green policy and deliver meaningful social good is what matters most to this segment.
Urban Hipsters members have a pretty positive Outlook on Life and see themselves as generally pretty lucky to enjoy the life they lead, which they think is better off than others. They are generally very good in health and lead an active and healthy lifestyle. Urban Hipsters members are optimistic about the future both for themselves and the world and look forward to what's ahead. 
The Values and Attitudes Dashboard provides a general overview of this fan segment's views and feelings towards sustainability, their outlook on life, politics and the cultural diversity of the segment.
Cultural Diversity refers to differences among people because of their racial or ethnic backgrounds, language, dress and traditions. The term cultural diversity can also refer to having different cultures respect each other's differences.
Sustainability means a focus on meeting our needs without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs. Sustainability is not just environmentalism it also includes concerns for social equity and economic development.
Political values are what people believe in that relates to how government should operate, whom it should serve or not serve, who pays for it and who benefits. This is normally referenced by a tendency to a political ideology.
Outlook on Life relates to a person's way of looking at things, their 'point of view' or attitude and how they perceive their life now and how positive they are towards the future, and the factors that impact the way they see the future.

All segments, except Urban Hipsters showed good growth as a percentage of 2024 numbers.
In regard to net number growth, Founding Faithfuls has the most new and/or returning members. 
The bulk of the movements were in and out of the Founding Faithfuls, Gen Fun and Urban Hipster segments.
As indicated earlier, Founding Faithfuls new and churning members were younger profile members. 
New or returning members exceeded lost, or churned members for all segments.

membership opportunities
Retention-Dependent Growth
The continued growth of Modern Families and Happy Suburbanites is essential for Fremantle's long-term membership stability.
As members age, they naturally migrate into Comfortable Crowd, Founding Faithfuls, or Breezy Boomers. Without strong retention in these middle-life segments, the pipeline into older loyal groups weakens.
Natural Decline of Older Cohorts
Segments like Breezy Boomers, Founding Faithfuls and (to a lesser extent) Comfortable Crowd include members reaching the later stages of the membership lifecycle and will experience gradual attrition over the next 10-20 years.
Unless actively replenished from younger feeder segments, the club risks a decline in overall stability and lifetime value.
Gen Fun & Urban Hipsters Can't Do It Alone
Despite good growth rates and strong inflows, both Gen Fun and Urban Hipsters are marked by higher churn percentages suggesting lower stickiness.
These segments are vital feeders into Fremantle's family and mid-life tiers but require sustained investment to improve retention and long-term engagement.
Experience-building and early value delivery will be key to ensuring these members don't lapse before progressing through the lifecycle.

key observations
Urban Hipsters are the most dynamic and volatile segment, with the highest churn and migration out but also the largest intake of new members. Despite strong recruitment, the segment experienced a net contraction, reinforcing the need for personalised onboarding and early engagement.
Modern Families and Happy Suburbanites are crucial growth bridges, showing high inbound migration and strong overall net growth. These are the engine rooms of Fremantle's mid-lifecycle fan base, benefiting from both migration and new acquisition.
Gen Fun is a high-potential development segment, with the second-highest new member rate. With a net growth, this segment is critical for capturing early fandom and building identity but requires nurture to avoid early drop-off.
Founding Faithfuls is a unique hybrid segment - skewing older but also housing younger adults in multi-generational households. It is one of Fremantle's most stable cohorts, with low migration and moderate churn, making it a reliable membership anchor. Its strength lies in loyalty and continuity, rather than growth - and it plays a vital role in maintaining generational links with the club.
Breezy Boomers is a stable, late-lifecycle segment, with modest churn and minimal migration out. Their net growth is largely supported by strong in-flows from Comfortable Crowd and Happy Suburbanites, rather than new member acquisition.

members on the move
Nurture & Develop segment
Urban Hipster is Fremantle's most dynamic segment; it's where most new members join, but also where the highest volume of churn occurs. This volatility highlights the need for targeted nurturing and development to improve retention and lift engagement.
These members typically show lower overall engagement: shorter tenure, reduced match attendance, and low interaction with club communications, including email open rates and survey participation.
Deeper consideration of the value proposition to this segment is required to ensure their broader more diverse needs and expectations are being met.
Outlook: Without sustained and personalised engagement, this group is at high risk of churn. However, when nurtured effectively, Urban Hipsters can become a valuable feeder segment for future Fremantle families such as Modern Families - with strong potential for long-term value.

members on the move
Growth segment
Modern Families often evolve from earlier-stage segments like Urban Hipster or (slightly less commonly) Gen Fun. As they settle into family life, they tend to progress into Happy Suburbanite or Comfortable Crowd segments over time but also have a high churn rate.
They currently represent 14% of the Fremantle base which is a smaller share than their representation in the broader WA and national population. Their engagement levels, including match attendance and responsiveness to marketing, sit below the club average.
Outlook: A growth-oriented segment with room to expand. Unlocking their value depends on bridging lifestyle needs with relevant products and communications, helping them stay engaged through key family life stages as they navigate competing priorities and stretched discretionary budgets, both in time and finances. Reducing churn in this segment is key to generating growth in members.

members on the move
Anchor segment
Happy Suburbanite members primarily arrive from Modern Families and Urban Hipster segments, but they also flow onward into older, more established groups like Comfortable Crowd (which is slightly older), Breezy Boomers and potentially Founding Faithful. While churn is relatively low, migration in and out is high - particularly inbound.
This is a transitional middle-aged segment that carries high financial and strategic value. It plays a dual role: contributing meaningfully to the club now, while also nurturing the next generation of long-term loyal fans.
Outlook: Members don't typically stay here long-term; it's a pipeline segment into Fremantle's older, more rusted-on supporter groups. Nonetheless, it remains critical for growing and stabilising the base during key life-stage transitions.

The Fremantle member base is growing, diverse, and increasingly polarised between loyalty and volatility. Seven segments show meaningfully different profiles across tenure, attendance, churn and commercial value. Overall membership grew in 2025, driven by strong new member acquisition across multiple segments - but this growth co-exists with a club baseline churn rate of 22% and a highest-segment churn rate of 47% (Urban Hipsters), creating a pattern of high-volume churn and high-volume replacement that is commercially inefficient and structurally fragile.
Value is concentrated in older, longer-tenured segments. Breezy Boomers and Comfortable Crowd carry the highest average value, and the longest average tenures. Their stability is real but finite - their contribution will naturally decline over time unless mid-lifecycle segments are actively retained and converted.
Engagement does not reliably track with value or tenure. Gen Fun shows the highest new member growth but the second-highest churn. Urban Hipsters attract the largest volume of new members but also recorded a net contraction - the only segment to do so. Email engagement ranges from 52% open rates in Breezy Boomers to just 19% in Gen Fun and 32% in Urban Hipsters, reinforcing that broadcast communications are ineffective across the segments that need them most.
Fremantle members over-index on commercially significant spend categories. Spend propensity data shows clear alignment across EV charging and vehicles, financial services, travel, premium dining, and insurance - categories that support targeted, evidence-based partner propositions that move the club beyond generic audience reach.
summary of findings

key observations
These observations surface the behaviours that matter most for driving retention, value growth and commercial outcomes across the Fremantle Dockers member base.
Engagement
Email performance is weakest precisely where it matters most. Gen Fun members open just 19% of emails and click at 2% - the lowest of all segments and a clear signal that broadcast communications are not reaching this cohort with relevant content or timing.
One in four members with game access attended no events in 2025. This non-attending cohort is concentrated in younger segments and is behaviourally indistinct from lapsed members - the difference is they are still paying.
Churn Risk
Urban Hipsters churned at 47% - more than double the club baseline of 22% - and recorded the only net contraction of any segment. New member volume is masking an underlying retention failure that will become harder to paper over as acquisition efficiency declines.
Gen Fun grew by 53% in net terms but carried 27% churn. High headline growth and high churn in the same segment is a warning sign: the base is being rebuilt annually rather than compounded.
Loyalty & Tenure
Breezy Boomers and Founding Faithfuls members are the club's retention anchors - both grew in 2025, driven by in-migration and securing new and returning lapsed members. Their churn rates are the two lowest of all segments. Their value is structural, not scalable.
Members who reach 10+ years tenure show dramatically lower churn propensity. The data consistently points to years 0-4 as the period that determines whether a new member becomes a long-term asset or a replacement cost.
Value & Share of Wallet
AMPPI indexing reveals a two-tier commercial base. Breezy Boomers and Comfortable Crowd sit at high value; Urban Hipsters and Gen Fun are the lowest-value segments and are also the highest-churn segments - a combination that makes them costly to retain and difficult to monetise.
Fremantle members over-index on EV, financial services, travel and premium lifestyle spend. This presents a targetable commercial profile that supports segment-aligned partnership propositions rather than broad reach conversations.

recommendations
Winning New Members | Focus acquisition on lookalike modelling against Founding Faithfuls and Happy Suburbanites - segments with proven lifecycle progression into the club's long-term loyal tiers. Chasing volume in the Urban Hipster profile generates turnover, not members.
Tailor acquisition messaging to segment motivation: belonging and tradition for Founding Faithfuls, family experience for Modern Families, social identity and access for Gen Fun. A single "become a member" narrative leaves value on the table.
Reducing Churn | Implement an early-tenure retention program for years 0-4 with clear behavioural triggers: zero attendance, declining email interaction, no merchandise purchase.
Build a non-match value proposition for non-attending members. With 24% of game-access members attending zero events, membership needs to feel worth keeping without a seat at the ground.
Increasing Share of Wallet | Design progression pathways by segment: GA to reserved seat, flexi to full season, single to family bundle. Modern Families and Happy Suburbanites present the clearest upsell opportunity but remain the least activated.
Time upgrade offers using behavioural signals - attendance streaks, merchandise activity, email clicks - not blanket renewal windows. Conversion is higher in the fortnight after a strong attendance than during a generic campaign.
The following recommendations are informed by the behaviours and patterns observed across the member base. They are not presented as definitive answers, but as a set of informed prompts designed to encourage discussion, prioritisation and alignment on where targeted action could deliver the greatest impact.
